test_suite.details: node test.js: 3519/3519 passed. Schema, internal-link, build-pipeline, prose, prediction-panel checks all green.

internal_anchors.details: test.js Section 3 covers all href="#…" targets and showTab() args. Spot-check confirmed all 12 showTab args resolve to one of 12 ds-tab-content divs; 0 args without matching tab.

tab_structure.details: Tab order overview→evaluate→model→selftest→wins→pages→killshots→timestamp→predictions→falsify→ai→refs is continuous. 12 ds-section-nav blocks (one per tab); first tab's prev=null/empty, last tab's next=null. Each tab has at least one h1 and one h2; heading hierarchy never skips a level.

external_links.details: DOI/publisher domains skipped per spec (Curmudgeon checks them per-WIN). Sample of 44 data-source URLs (NOAA, USGS, ESA, NASA, INTERMAGNET, GitHub, Wikipedia, IAU, GFZ, IVS, Cosmos.esa.int, BGS, kp.gfz, dlmf.nist, simbad, opentimestamps): 41 returned 200 with browser UA, 1 (podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov path) TCP-timed-out twice, 2 NOAA paths returned 403 to default UA but 200 with browser UA (Akamai/CDN bot policy — real users get the page). The podaac-tools timeout is host-specific; podaac.jpl.nasa.gov base host returns 200. Carry-forward from prior runs.

mismatches.location: data/sections.json part1b TLDR; rendered at docs/index.html line 1027 ('The 30 new WINs break into recognizable patterns'). The detail body two paragraphs later correctly templates {{NEW_IN_V51}} → 32, so the same <details> block contradicts itself.
mismatches.additional: The breakdown sums (9+5+5+9=28) also do not reach 32 — the TLDR count AND the per-bucket numbers both need refresh.

data_prose_consistency.details: Total wins.json = 71 entries (69 base + 2 sub-IDs WIN-057b/058b). Verdict-bar counts match exactly: Misleading 22, Std Model 18, Self-Contradicted 14, Refuted 9, Not Demonstrated 4, Unfalsifiable 4 (sum = 71, all percentages computed against 71). Scorecard cards 69/0/4/4 use the dome's own claim count and dome_claimed_failures from uncounted-failures.json. new_in_v51 actual = 32 (WIN-040..069 plus WIN-057b/058b). Same stale TLDR flagged in 2026-05-04T05-39 report — not yet fixed.

win_detail_consistency.details: All 71 expected winNNN[suffix] anchors found in HTML. WIN-057b and WIN-058b correctly track the dome's number-collision. 8 detail_groups internally consistent. All wins have tldr_evidence + tldr_verdict; all 12 genuinely-prospective predictions have a tldr.

win_alignment_with_dome_site.details: Fetched john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html. WIN-001..WIN-069 enumerated (69 unique IDs). WIN-057 and WIN-058 each carry two distinct claim definitions on the dome side; our 057/057b/058/058b mappings cover both. PROS-001..PROS-008 cross-references noted but not in scope.

progressive_disclosure.details: 351 <details>, 347 <summary> blocks. ps-summary 119 / ks-summary 228 = 347. ps-tldr 119 / ks-tldr 228 = 347. 0 empty TLDRs. h2 elements: 225 total, 193 with display:inline (canonical inside-summary form). Remaining h2 are scorecard headers and section-level titles — legitimate per-prompt exceptions.

theme_color_drift.details: 0 hardcoded light backgrounds (#fff, #f8f9fa, etc.) in sections.json inline styles. 3 occurrences of color:#555 in inline italic-subheader styles (sections.json part4.html, part5.html, part6.html) — minor, could be replaced with var(--text) but does not break dark theme. Carry-forward from prior runs.

discoverability_infrastructure.details: docs/llms.txt (5,628 B) opens with our review URL framing, mentions the dome model by name, references current 71/69 counts. docs/sitemap.xml is valid XML with main URL + llms.txt URL. docs/robots.txt has Allow: / and Sitemap: directive pointing to our sitemap. <head> meta: description, og:title, og:description, ld+json ClaimReview block all present. data/uncounted-failures.json: 10 entries, all with id/dome_ref/dome_label/what_actually_happened, no duplicates, all FAIL-NNN format. dome_claimed_failures=4 matches scorecard.

expansion_tracker_continuity.details: Cloned git fresh and compared against FUSE workspace. GIT canonical: next_id=286, max_id=285, 284 items — OK. FUSE: next_id=285, max_id=285 — would collide on next allocation. md5sum confirms FUSE file differs from git (FUSE 1f1e101…, git c32071a…). FUSE expansion-tracker.json mtime 2026-05-03 05:17, git mtime via clone 2026-05-04 08:15. Likely cause: decider committed the next_id=286 update directly to git in its own clone, but workspace-sync has not run since 2026-05-03 05:06 (see workspace_sync_status check), so the corrected file has not propagated back to FUSE. The git state is correct; the FUSE staleness is the symptom of workspace-sync being stuck. Single ID gap at EXP-052 is long-standing (carry-forward). 276 EXP-NNN files in expansions/, 0 strict orphans, 0 phantoms, 0 stale-pending. 95 non-EXP files (ISS-*, MODE5-*, PRED-assessment-*) are legitimate artifacts from other pipelines. 0 coupled-category items need queue presence.

workspace_only_files.details: WIN-016.c3.json (the major-but-transient finding from 2026-05-04T05-39 run) has been committed to git (mtime in clone 2026-05-04 08:15). Workspace has 455 review files vs git's 454 — the only diff is WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (empty stray, name-mangled debris from a node script — not data). Workspace expansions/ has 1 extra file: EXP-202.json.bak (canonical EXP-202.json present in git, identical size). Both debris files are carry-forward; not data-loss-critical, but recommend cleanup.

workspace_sync_status.details: workspace-sync has not run since 2026-05-03T05:06:40Z (last entry in monitor/integrity/workspace-sync-runs/). Current time 2026-05-04T08:20Z = ~27h 13m since last run. Schedule is every 4h, so ~6 runs missed. Last skip-log entry timestamp 2026-05-03T04:05Z (~28.2h ago). This is the proximate cause of FUSE staleness on expansion-tracker.json (next_id 285 on FUSE vs 286 on git). Out of strict scope for structure-integrity but reported because (a) the prompt's section 7d defines the skip-log check and (b) the staleness is producing a downstream WARN on expansion_tracker_continuity. Recommend tinker investigate why dome-workspace-sync isn't firing.

workspace_sync_skip_log.details: 0 skips in last 24h window — but only because the agent has not run. Last log entry 2026-05-03T04:05Z. 0 direction-violation skips total since prior report; 0 mtime-guard skips. No boundary-crossing bugs detectable from the log; staleness has different root cause (agent dormant).

digest_coverage.details: Regenerated pending-digest.json before reading. Total review files: 454, unique base IDs (latest cycle): 248. Processed: 447. Unprocessed: 2 (WIN-014.c4.json, WIN-016.c3.json) — both very recent (within normal pipeline lag, decider has not re-processed since their commits). 0 parse errors. Side note from digest: 233 'under-covered processed' reviews flagging 1256 missing-issues holes — informational, not in this prompt's scope, surfaced for decider/tinker.

project_documentation.details: All 16 spot-checked file references in CLAUDE.md exist on disk. The bare prompt-filename references (analyst.md, decider.md, etc.) are short-form aliases for monitor/prompts/<name>.md and all resolve. Schedule table well-formed (8 agents enumerated). The single bare 69 count ('the model claims 69 confirmed predictions') is the dome's own claim, accompanied by the live query for our review's count, so it complies with the no-hardcoded-counts rule.

build_reproducibility.details: Cloned fresh to /tmp/dome-integrity-clone and ran node build.js html. Output byte-identical to committed docs/index.html (md5 308279444022f6faf81aec10a68b917e). Also identical to FUSE copy. git diff after build: nothing to commit. Last build was on May 4 (today), so the build-date stamp is in sync. Clone cleaned up after run.

issues_found.description: data/sections.json part1b 'new-content-breakdown' TLDR says 'The 30 new WINs break into recognizable patterns' — actual new_in_v51 = 32. The detail body two paragraphs later correctly templates {{NEW_IN_V51}} = 32, so the same <details> block is internally inconsistent. Also the sub-bucket sums (9+5+5+9 = 28) do not reach 32 — needs both the TLDR count AND the breakdown numbers refreshed against the current set: WIN-040..069 + WIN-057b/058b = 32.
issues_found.location: data/sections.json part1b html (rendered at docs/index.html line 1027)
issues_found.suggested_fix: Replace 'The 30 new WINs' → 'The {{NEW_IN_V51}} new WINs' and recompute the per-bucket sums from wins.json so they actually total 32. Same finding as 2026-05-04T05-39 run; not yet acted on.

issues_found.description: FUSE workspace has expansion-tracker.json at next_id=285, max_id=285 (would collide on next allocation). Git canonical has next_id=286 (correct). md5 differs. The git state is fine; FUSE is the stale side. Risk: if any agent allocates from FUSE, it will read 285 and write a colliding EXP-285. Mitigated by analyst's GitHub raw-URL cross-check, but the staleness signals workspace-sync has stopped propagating (see workspace_sync_status).
issues_found.suggested_fix: Restore workspace-sync (see next finding); alternatively the decider's next push-and-copy-to-FUSE block will overwrite FUSE.

issues_found.description: dome-workspace-sync agent has not run since 2026-05-03T05:06:40Z (~27h 13m ago). Schedule is every 4 hours, so ~6 runs missed. This is the root cause of the FUSE staleness above. Other agents (decider, curmudgeon, analyst) appear to be functioning — the integrity report from 2026-05-04T05-39 was produced normally, and WIN-016.c3.json was committed to git overnight.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Tinker should investigate why the sync agent is stuck. Until restored, FUSE will continue to drift from git on multi-writer files (expansion-tracker.json, review-state.json, attention-inbox.json).

issues_found.description: podaac-tools.jpl.nasa.gov GRACE ProdSpec PDF — TCP timeout twice from this network (≥20 s). Other podaac.jpl.nasa.gov URLs return 200, so it's host-specific. Possibly migrated; recheck next run.

monitor/curmudgeon/reviews/WIN-067.c3.json.build.js (empty 0-byte stray) and monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json.bak (redundant — canonical EXP-202.json present in git, identical 41,999 B). Both carry-forward debris.
issues_found.suggested_fix: rm both files. The .build.js name-mangled stray suggests a curmudgeon-side path-construction bug worth checking in monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md.

issues_found.description: 3 hardcoded color:#555 in inline italic-subheader styles (sections.json part4.html, part5.html, part6.html). Could be replaced with var(--text); doesn't break dark theme.
EXP-052 ID gap (carry-forward, single skipped allocation, next_id correct in git). Long-standing.

summary: Site is healthy and the build is reproducible byte-for-byte. The story this run is FUSE staleness driven by dome-workspace-sync being dormant for ~27h. Key consequence: FUSE expansion-tracker.json shows next_id=285 (would collide), but the actual git canonical state is next_id=286 (correct). No data loss, but multi-writer files will continue to drift on FUSE until workspace-sync resumes. The May-3 stale 'The 30 new WINs' TLDR is unfixed since this morning's run. Test suite pass 3,519/3,519. Build diff zero lines. WIN-016.c3.json (transient flag from prior run) is now in git. No critical issues.
